diff_summary.commands/close.md: Unstaged working-tree change at line 113: replaced inline backtick `rm -f '/tmp/claude-close-force-<DEV_SESSION_ID>.flag' 2>/dev/null || true` with prose reference to `scripts/cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh`. Also committed earlier in this cycle (commit f79255f): branch-number corrections at lines 330 and 334, and the qa-report skip clause at the forced-path task-id resolution section.
diff_summary.commands/do.md: Committed (chore/orphan 07d6e32): added ## Closing /do-developed work section (lines 8-27) documenting escape-hatch instructions for /do-developed work.
diff_summary.scripts/cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh: New untracked file: 13-line bash script that accepts a sentinel file path as $1 and runs rm -f (errors swallowed). Created to resolve the no-inline-code violation at close.md:113.

standards_checked.name: no-inline-code
standards_checked.notes: Working-tree close.md:113 references scripts/cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh by prose — no inline bash. The HEAD version still contains the inline rm -f form; the fix is unstaged. do.md code blocks (lines 14-16, 24-26) are user-facing /close and /commit invocation examples — these are usage examples, not executable logic. cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh is a real script file.

standards_checked.name: no-hardcoded-values
standards_checked.notes: cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh uses ${1:-} positional parameter for the sentinel path — no hardcoded path. close.md references /tmp/claude-close-force-<DEV_SESSION_ID>.flag as a template placeholder only (angle-bracket variable, not a literal value). do.md has no hardcoded paths or values.

standards_checked.name: use-source-venv
standards_checked.notes: No python3 calls in any of the three changed files.

standards_checked.name: integer-step-numbering
standards_checked.notes: No decimal (e.g., 4.5) or letter (e.g., 4a) step numbering introduced by the diff in any of the three files. The existing numbered sub-bullets in close.md (e.g., (a), (b), (c) within step bodies) are sub-clause labels within a prose rule, not step numbers.

standards_checked.name: meaningful-naming
standards_checked.notes: cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh is a descriptive, purpose-specific name (cleanup + close-force + sentinel). No enhance-/fast-/v2-/optimize- patterns.

standards_checked.name: english-only
standards_checked.notes: No non-English characters in any of the three changed files. The ticket source contains Chinese (user-verbatim quotes in the requirement table), but that is docs/ scope and excluded from inspection.

standards_checked.name: merge-similar-scripts
standards_checked.notes: cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh does not duplicate any existing script. Closest neighbors: cleanup-tests-folder.sh (different domain), write-codex-enforce.sh and write-e2e-enforce.sh (write files, not remove sentinel flags). The new script purpose — remove a specific /tmp flag file whose path is resolved at runtime — is unique.

standards_checked.name: concise-documentation
standards_checked.notes: do.md ## Closing /do-developed work section is 20 lines, concise, no marketing language, two short code examples (the /close and /commit invocations). cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh has a 2-line comment header. close.md:113 change is a single sentence substitution. No verbosity introduced.

standards_checked.name: parameterized-scripts
standards_checked.notes: cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh accepts the sentinel path via $1 with an explicit usage check (exits 1 if empty). No values are hardcoded inside the script body.

standards_checked.name: dangling-references
standards_checked.notes: scripts/cleanup-close-force-sentinel.sh is referenced at close.md:113 and exists on disk as an untracked new file (confirmed). Pre-existing references in close.md: /root/.claude/scripts/todo/close.py and /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py both confirmed to exist. do.md introduces no new script references.

standards_checked.name: merge-conflicts
standards_checked.notes: No merge conflict markers (<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) in any of the three files.
